page: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 virtual CPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng testing, while multi-core performance falls into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks show weak performance in the bottom 25%, though overall PassMark CPU and memory scores remain average. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as single-threaded gzip compression and PassMark CPU encryption, demonstrate average performance, whereas database operations and Redis SET operations register in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6a.xlarge offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads without the premium of specialized compute or memory-optimized hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and the modest 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth represent clear tradeoffs for I/O-intensive applications. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, light web serving, small-scale application hosting, and basic background processing tasks that do not require high multi-threaded throughput or massive memory bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs (2 physical cores), 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower quartiles. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform at average levels, while database and Redis operations show weaker throughput. Qualitatively, the instance provides a balanced resource profile for standard workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, light web serving, and small-scale development environments that do not demand high multi-threaded performance or intensive local storage I/O.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, delivering balanced resources for standard workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and DDR4 memory for standard, non-intensive business workloads.

16.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmarks
No local storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for general-purpose applications and light web serving
